topic: A technician performing a 'Three-Point Bend' test on a carbon fiber sample.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: Front view: The technician places the carbon fiber sample on the three-point bending fixture, aligning the center with the supports.
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: Close-up: The loading nose descends to contact the sample as the supports hold the ends steady.
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: Side-angle: The technician watches the midspan deflection while the load increases toward the fracture threshold.
parsed_generation.narration: The technician secures the carbon fiber sample in the three-point bend fixture and aligns it for testing. The loading nose engages and midspan deflection is observed as the load increases.

feedback: The sequence captures the three-point bending setup and a camera-focused progression, but it lacks the explicit fracture moment and fixture details emphasized in the gold standard. Consider aligning the shot structure to two clear intervals [00:00-00:04] and [00:04-00:08], show the carbon fiber on metal supports under vertical load, and include a definitive fracture cue. Streamline shot labels to mirror the gold standard’s bracketed format and enhance lighting/texture cues for visuals.
strengths: Clear identification of three-shot sequence and camera angles
Covers loading contact and midspan deflection
fixes: Adopt two-interval timing to match gold standard
Include explicit fracture event and fixture details
Adjust wording to match concise, bracketed timestamp style without 'Shot' labels
